model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Under a fixed 256-step budget, this compact CNN is strongly optimization-limited early in training. A higher Adam learning rate is a simple, high-leverage change that typically improves loss within short budgets without adding code complexity.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"The prior verifier result showed the cosine schedule over the short 256-step budget degraded validation loss, so reverting to a constant learning rate is the safest high-value correction before testing new ideas.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"With only two pooled conv blocks, the model is capacity-limited. Increasing the stem width from 12 to 16 is a simple architecture change that can improve feature quality within the same optimization setup and step budget.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The wider model regressed under the fixed short-step budget, indicating optimization/capacity mismatch. Reverting channel width restores the better-performing compact regime before further tuning.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.0","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"In a very short 256-step regime, L2 regularization can underfit early optimization. Removing weight decay is a minimal change that may lower validation loss by allowing faster fit.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.0","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Under fixed-step evaluation, increasing batch size doubles unique training examples seen within 256 updates, which can materially improve short-budget validation loss. Restoring prior weight decay avoids the regression observed when regularization was removed.","secondary_modes":["caching","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"With fixed update count, increasing batch size further increases sample coverage per run and can improve early-loss efficiency; this is a minimal extension of the successful step-6 direction.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After increasing batch size to 256, a moderately higher learning rate is a natural scaling adjustment and can improve convergence within the same fixed 256 update budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"At larger-batch training, decoupled weight decay can improve optimization/generalization balance versus Adam with coupled L2, while keeping the rest of the successful configuration unchanged.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"AdamW regressed on this workload under the current short-budget setting, so reverting to Adam restores the stronger optimizer before further tuning.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The move to 2e-3 improved loss at batch size 256; testing a further moderate LR increase is a direct continuation of the same successful scaling trend under fixed-step training.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The higher 2.5e-3 learning rate regressed, so reverting to 2e-3 restores the current best-performing learning-rate setting for this batch regime.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"For short-budget training, lowering Adam beta2 can make second-moment estimates adapt faster and sometimes improve early convergence at fixed step count.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Lowering beta2 regressed validation loss, so restoring the previous Adam betas returns to the stronger optimizer dynamics.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"A moderate increase beyond 256 may further stabilize gradients in the fixed-step regime without the extreme jump to very large batches, making it a reasonable late-stage batch-size probe.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Batch-size scaling has consistently improved loss in this fixed-step setup; moving from 384 to 512 is the next direct test to increase sample coverage and reduce gradient noise further.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Batch size 512 regressed relative to 384, so reverting restores the current best-performing batch regime.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.8e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"At the stronger 384-batch regime, a slightly lower learning rate may improve final-step refinement and validation loss by reducing overshooting while keeping the successful configuration intact.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.8e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The 1.8e-3 trial regressed, so reverting to 2e-3 restores the strongest observed configuration paired with batch size 384.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}
